questions.explanation: NOT flips the input: Z = X̄. If X=0, Z=1; if X=1, Z=0. Also called negation or inversion.

questions.prompt: A 2-input OR gate outputs 0 only when:
questions.explanation: OR outputs 1 if ANY input is 1. The only case producing 0 is when ALL inputs are 0.

questions.prompt: A 2-input AND gate outputs 1 only when:
questions.explanation: AND outputs 1 only when ALL inputs are 1. Any single 0 input forces the output to 0.

questions.prompt: In a BJT transistor inverter, when the input is 0V:
questions.explanation: With 0V input, the base-emitter junction is not forward biased, so the transistor is in cutoff. No collector current flows, and the output is pulled to V_CC (5V) through the collector resistor.

questions.prompt: The diode OR circuit has an output of ~4.3V instead of 5V because:
options: The diode has a ~0.7V forward voltage drop
questions.correctAnswer: The diode has a ~0.7V forward voltage drop
questions.explanation: A silicon diode drops ~0.7V when forward biased. So 5V input → 5V – 0.7V = 4.3V at the output. This is a limitation of simple diode logic.

questions.prompt: The expression Z = X₀ + X₁ represents:
questions.explanation: In Boolean algebra, '+' means logical OR, not arithmetic addition. Z = X₀ + X₁ means Z is 1 if X₀ OR X₁ (or both) is 1.

questions.prompt: The expression Z = X₀ · X₁ represents:
questions.explanation: In Boolean algebra, '·' (dot) means logical AND. Z = X₀ · X₁ means Z is 1 only when BOTH X₀ AND X₁ are 1.

questions.prompt: Why is diode-only logic not used for more than 1-2 stages?
options: Diodes can only handle one input
questions.explanation: Each diode stage loses ~0.7V. After several stages, the voltage degrades below the logic threshold. Real logic families use transistors for signal restoration at every gate.

questions.prompt: In TTL logic, the typical HIGH voltage is:
questions.explanation: Standard TTL uses 5V for logic HIGH and 0V for logic LOW. Though modern systems use 3.3V or 1.8V, the original TTL standard is 5V.

questions.prompt: The three basic logic operations are:
questions.explanation: NOT (complement), OR (disjunction), and AND (conjunction) are the three primitive operations. All other logic functions can be built from these three.
